blocks.text: Antibodies (immunoglobulins) are Y- shaped proteins produced by B cells.

lines.text: shaped proteins produced by B cells.

blocks.text: • Heavy Chains: Two identical long polypeptide chains.
• Light Chains: Two identical short chains.
• Variable Region (Fab): The tips of the Y, responsible for antigen binding (highly specific).
• Constant Region (Fc): The stem, dictates the antibody's isotype (IgG, IgA, IgM, etc.) and immune [Cite: "Cellular and Molecular Immunology, Abbas"] function.
